memories.value: First run. Discover all properties and their current listing descriptions across channels. Audit listing completeness and identify content gaps. Establish content calendar for the next quarter.

Airbnb listing optimization: Title limited to 50 chars — lead with unique selling point ('Oceanfront Cottage' not 'Nice Beach House'). First 3 lines of description are visible without clicking 'Read more' — put best content there. Use all 5 highlight sections. Mention neighborhood, walkability, and proximity to attractions. Update photos seasonally.

VRBO listing optimization: Headline can be longer (80 chars). Emphasize family-friendly features: safety gates, high chairs, game rooms, pool fences. Detail the full amenity list — VRBO guests search by amenities more than Airbnb guests. Include accurate square footage. Highlight nearby family activities.

Direct booking SEO: Use location + property type keywords in title and URL slug ('myrtle-beach-oceanfront-condo'). Write 300+ word descriptions with natural keyword usage. Include FAQ section for long-tail search queries. Blog posts about local attractions drive organic traffic to direct booking site.

Facebook Marketplace tips: Price must be the first thing visible. Use bright, high-quality hero photo (living room or outdoor space, not bedroom). Keep description under 200 words — casual tone, bullet points. Re-post every 2 weeks to refresh algorithm visibility. Respond to comments quickly — engagement boosts reach.

No content calendar established yet. Will create quarterly content plan aligned with seasonal demand: pre-season listing refreshes, seasonal promotion campaigns, holiday-specific content, off-season deal pushes.

Photo requirements per platform: Airbnb minimum 5, recommended 30+ (including 1 cover photo at 3:2 ratio). VRBO minimum 6, recommended 25+ (1 hero image). All platforms: natural lighting, wide-angle shots, show every room, include exterior and neighborhood. Seasonal photo updates improve click-through rates by 15-25%.
